issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://multiup.io/download/f96daf5bc2673fd7dbe901a46727ebbe/Wolventhrone-The_Siege_Of_Sorrows_Gate-WEB-2025-FiH.rar](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmultiup.io%2Fdownload%2Ff96daf5bc2673fd7dbe901a46727ebbe%2FWolventhrone-The_Siege_Of_Sorrows_Gate-WEB-2025-FiH.rar) ### Comment > if you click Download, instead of getting you (after a cloudflare verification) to the actual multiup page with various mirrors, most of the times it gets you to one of the following fake/malware "Your file is ready to download" pages: > > ``` > https://file274751.cloud06x.cfd/ > https://file996100.cloud06x.cfd/ > https://file889833.cloud06x.cfd/ > ``` > > <br/> > PS. In this 'Create public GitHub issue' form, in the "Share your settings" page, the "AdGuard Annoyances filter" that's listed, does not exist in AdGuard extension|Settings|Filters|Annoyances. ``` Username: @darkred ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/g5513oqgax44wogw8gkcowgssokkssw6r2581udfaw.jpg?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.31 System version: | `Windows 11, version 25H2` Browser: | Chrome Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

Hide your search queries,
Send Do-Not-Track header

**Miscellaneous**:
Remove X-Client-Data header from HTTP requests Browsing Security: | disabled Browsing Security statistics: | enabled ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=ext&product_version=5.3.1.31&system_version=Windows+11%2C+version+25H2&browser=chrome&filters=2.3.18.19.20.21.22.208.257&browsing_security.enabled=false&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=true&stealth.enabled=true&stealth.hide_search_queries=true&stealth.DNT=true&stealth.x_client=true&stealth.block_trackers=true)

issue.title: originality.ai
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://originality.ai/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Foriginality.ai%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Team, > I have a few questions about how the extension and the reporting system work: > [1] Why is blocking a simple cookie banner dependent on the "Annoyances filter"? What is the purpose of having a specific "AdGuard Cookie Notices filter" if it doesn't work independently? > [2] Why is the reporting tool forcing me to enable the "AdGuard Annoyances filter" just to submit a report? I am currently using "Dandelion Sprout's Annoyances List," yet the system still blocks my submission. This makes no sense. > [3] My AdGuard extension DOESN'T EVEN HAVE a filter named "AdGuard Annoyances filter" in the settings. > I hope you can streamline this process and fix the filtering logic, as the reporting hurdles are currently more annoying than the cookies themselves. > Thanks! ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/dr5pn9r2ee8gcsww4gsgoc0sg0o00koapaeq5m0vd4.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/5u3i115t5hsso008go4wkks4k008o4saw00cb72new.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.7 MV2 System version: | `Windows 10` Browser: | Edge Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Annoyances:**
AdGuard Cookie Notices filter,
AdGuard Popups filter,
Dandelion Sprout's Annoyances List,

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://originality.ai/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Foriginality.ai%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Team, > I have a few questions about how the extension and the reporting system work: > [1] Why is blocking a simple cookie banner dependent on the "Annoyances filter"? What is the purpose of having a specific "AdGuard Cookie Notices filter" if it doesn't work independently? > [2] Why is the reporting tool forcing me to enable the "AdGuard Annoyances filter" just to submit a report? I am currently using "Dandelion Sprout's Annoyances List," yet the system still blocks my submission. This makes no sense. > [3] My AdGuard extension DOESN'T EVEN HAVE a filter named "AdGuard Annoyances filter" in the settings. > I hope you can streamline this process and fix the filtering logic, as the reporting hurdles are currently more annoying than the cookies themselves. > Thanks! ### Screenshots

comment.body:
I'm suspecting:
1) AdGuard's Safe Browsing database uses Seclookup as one of its secret sources, and
2) Seclookup is acting strange recently and is falsely marking various small sites at apparently random.

comment.body:
Hi, to clarify on the Google Search bug, I meant that this was a Google issue that occurred for a short time, and was unrelated to AdGuard.  But since it caused the same result to occur, it was very easy to be confused w/ the Other Annoyances filter issue.

I posted it on the Google Help forum, and submitted a bug report via the Google “Send Feedback” link at the bottom of the search pages.  If there is a more direct, and better way to submit issues to them (such as a developer site like Github), please let me know.

issue.title: Fix #228225 smplu.link

comment.body:
Could also just do something like this. It leaves a small leftover, but that's good because that's what they're checking for.
```
#%#//scriptlet('remove-node-text', '#text', '🚀 GET FAST VPN (VLESS)')
#%#//scriptlet('remove-node-text', '#text', '🔐 PRIVATE TELEGRAM PROXY')
```
It doesn't seem to remove the first one when you're on the proxy page.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://originality.ai/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Foriginality.ai%2F) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Team, > I have a few questions about how the extension and the reporting system work: > [1] Why is blocking a simple cookie banner dependent on the "Annoyances filter"? What is the purpose of having a specific "AdGuard Cookie Notices filter" if it doesn't work independently? > [2] Why is the reporting tool forcing me to enable the "AdGuard Annoyances filter" just to submit a report? I am currently using "Dandelion Sprout's Annoyances List," yet the system still blocks my submission. This makes no sense. > [3] My AdGuard extension DOESN'T EVEN HAVE a filter named "AdGuard Annoyances filter" in the settings. > I hope you can streamline this process and fix the filtering logic, as the reporting hurdles are currently more annoying than the cookies themselves. > Thanks! ### Screenshots

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
4. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
5. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
6. Tap to install
7. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
8. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).
